pending.body: finding_summary: `reconcile_success()` now blocks idempotent replay even when no new bead mutation is needed. The new preflight at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:244-246` runs before `close_bead_idempotent()`, but that helper is explicitly read-first and returns early when `br show` says the bead is already closed (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:424-426`). Even if that initial guard were moved, `sync_after_close()` still unconditionally health-gates and runs `sync_flush()` at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:459-467` instead of using the new `sync_if_dirty()` path. In a crash-recovery replay where the prior close+sync already succeeded, an unrelated later conflict/malformed `.beads/issues.jsonl` now prevents milestone-state completion even though there is nothing left to mutate or flush. severity: medium. affected_files: ["src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs"]. remediation_hint: only gate the branch that will actually call `close_bead`, and use `sync_if_dirty()`/pending-mutation state for replay so already-closed, already-synced recoveries can still advance milestone state without requiring a clean export.

finding_summary: `claim_bead_in_br()` skips the new health recheck on the recovered-claim short-circuit path. After `sync_if_dirty()` succeeds, lines `src/cli/project.rs:984-989` immediately return `Ok(())` when the recovered flush replayed `update_bead_status(<bead>, "in_progress")`. Unlike the normal explicit-claim path, which rechecks export health before its final sync at `src/cli/project.rs:992-1002`, this early return never verifies that the recovered `br sync --flush-only` left `.beads/issues.jsonl` in a safe state. If the replayed flush itself leaves conflict markers or malformed JSONL behind, the controller records a successful claim and proceeds with a corrupted bead export. severity: medium. affected_files: ["src/cli/project.rs"]. remediation_hint: run the same post-flush health guard before returning from the recovered-claim path, and add a regression test where the replayed `sync --flush-only` both replays the claim and corrupts `.beads/issues.jsonl`.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 14 -> Plan and Implement
